title: wolfhsm: implement signature::Signer for ECC and Ed25519 keys
description:
## Why
The plan called for `EccP256Signer` and `Ed25519Signer` types implementing the RustCrypto `signature::Signer` trait. These are absent from the implementation. Without the trait impl, wolfhsm key types cannot be used as drop-in signers in any generic context (e.g. `fn sign_jwt<S: Signer<DerSignature>>(s: &S, ...)`). This is the biggest missing composability piece for RustCrypto users.

## What needs to be done
- Add `EccP256Signer<'a>{ key: &'a EccP256Key, client: &'a mut Client }` and `impl signature::Signer<p256::ecdsa::DerSignature> for EccP256Signer<'a>` in `crypto/ecc.rs`
- Add `Ed25519Signer<'a>` and `impl signature::Signer<ed25519::Signature> for Ed25519Signer<'a>` in `crypto/ed25519.rs`
- Add `EccP256Key::signer<'a>(&'a self, client: &'a mut Client) -> EccP256Signer<'a>` and same for Ed25519Key
- The ECC signer must hash the message with SHA-256 in Rust before calling `sign_digest`

## Acceptance criteria
- Both signer types satisfy the `signature::Signer` bound
- Test: `EccP256Key::signer()` can be passed to a generic function bounded on `Signer`
- Test: `Ed25519Key::signer()` same
